task: I need to put together a simple handoff note for a customer issue that’s been bouncing around a bit, and I want it to be easy for the next person to follow. What’s the cleanest way to lay out the status, what’s already been checked, and what still needs attention without making it too wordy?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it in a **short, fixed format** so the next person can scan it fast.

### Best layout
- **Issue:** what the customer is asking about
- **Status:** where it stands now
- **Checked:** what’s already been verified
- **Still needed:** what hasn’t been confirmed yet
- **Next step:** what the next person should do
- **Owner / due:** who has it and when it needs follow-up

### Example
**Issue:** Customer says delivery was late and wants an explanation.  
**Status:** Delay confirmed; cause still being checked.  
**Checked:** Delivery time, dispatch note, and carrier scan reviewed.  
**Still needed:** Confirmation from warehouse on release time.  
**Next step:** Follow up with warehouse and update customer.  
**Owner / due:** [Name] / [time]

### Good rules
- Stick to facts
- Don’t restate the whole history
- Don’t guess at the cause
- Keep each line to one sentence if you can

If you want, I can turn this into a copy-paste handoff template with blanks.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that format works. Clean enough.

I’d want one more thing in there though — **customer impact / urgency**. Sometimes that matters more than the backstory.

So I’d lay it out like this:
- **Issue:** what’s wrong
- **Status:** current stage
- **Checked:** what’s been verified
- **Still needed:** what’s missing
- **Impact / urgency:** is the customer waiting, blocked, escalating, etc.
- **Next step:** what happens next
- **Owner / due:** who’s on it and when

Keep it factual. No long history, no guessing. If there’s a dispute, note what’s documented and what isn’t.

If you want, I can tighten that into a one-line-per-field template you can copy into a case note.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — the cleanest way is to keep it in a fixed, one-line-per-field format so the next person can scan it fast.

### Simple handoff note format
- **Issue:** what the customer is asking about
- **Status:** where it stands now
- **Checked:** what’s already been verified
- **Still needed:** what hasn’t been confirmed yet
- **Impact / urgency:** whether the customer is waiting, blocked, or escalating
- **Next step:** what the next person should do
- **Owner / due:** who has it and when it needs follow-up

### Example
**Issue:** Customer says delivery was late and wants an explanation.  
**Status:** Delay confirmed; cause still being checked.  
**Checked:** Delivery time, dispatch note, and carrier scan reviewed.  
**Still needed:** Confirmation from warehouse on release time.  
**Impact / urgency:** Customer is waiting and may escalate today.  
**Next step:** Follow up with warehouse and update customer.  
**Owner / due:** [Name] / [time]

### Good rules
- Stick to facts
- Don’t guess at the cause
- Don’t restate the whole history
- Keep each field short and direct

If you want, I can turn this into a copy-paste template with blanks.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right structure. Plain, usable, not a lot of fluff. Adding **impact / urgency** makes sense too — that’s usually what tells the next person whether it’s just a follow-up or something that needs attention now.

That format would work fine for me:
- Issue
- Status
- Checked
- Still needed
- Impact / urgency
- Next step
- Owner / due

Short lines. Facts only. If there’s a dispute, note what’s confirmed and what isn’t. That’s clean enough to hand off without making the next person dig through a wall of text.
